rag_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be placed in component type scopes for assumptions/guarantees and implementation scopes for bindings.
Implementation-level signal routing contracts should be placed in the implementation annex after relevant subcomponents and connections.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL architectural structure when inserting AGREE annexes.
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model in AGREE contracts.
Component type contracts should reference ports through valid variables or outputs, not subcomponent ports directly.
Do not encode AADL properties as AGREE variables unless explicit typed ports or constants are available.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Implementation-level bindings require valid scope and must reference existing subcomponents and connections.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level signal routing in component type scopes unless explicitly required.
Do not reference subcomponent ports directly in component type contracts without proper variable representation.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
Model analyst extraction of AADL structure
